claim_status: No-claim profile/session schema-package template only; no executable profile schema, no Space schema, no session schema, no snapshot schema, no migration schema, no real-profile migration claim, no sync claim, no credential-storage claim, no data-loss safety claim, no user-data handling readiness claim, no production profile-format claim, and no implementation claim.

package_status.current_scope: Template and rejection rules for a future executable profile/session schema package only; it records no executable schema, no migration test, no fault test, no real-profile fixture approval, and no owner-approved production format evidence.

migration_lifecycle.required_record: Classify the source profile, Space, session, snapshot, or migration record without opening real user profile data outside the approved fixture policy.
Validate schema version, identity fields, checksums, privacy labels, downgrade policy, and bounded record sizes before any write.
Record the target schema, step plan, rollback plan, protected-work handling, privacy review status, and unsupported boundaries before execution.
Write a bounded temporary snapshot with checksum, retention, redaction, and cleanup records before commit.
Commit only after verification succeeds; otherwise rollback to the previous readable state without discarding protected work.
Repair only explicitly supported cases and quarantine corrupt, downgrade, private-session, or ambiguous data without accepting it as success.
Verify private-session exclusion, fake credential use, redacted diagnostics, bounded temporary root cleanup, and no real user profile data retention.

rejection_rules.condition: Reject any schema-package promotion when profile, Space, session, snapshot, or migration executable schemas are missing.
Reject success-only package evidence that omits malformed-input, interruption, rollback, repair, downgrade, privacy, and data-loss tests.
Reject schemas or migrations that silently discard protected work, unsaved form state, downloads, uploads, or pending consequential agent actions.
Reject fixtures or schemas that allow private-session data to enter normal profile, session, snapshot, export, trace, or diagnostic records.
Reject corrupt, truncated, duplicated, reordered, or checksum-mismatched records when they are accepted as successful migrations.
Reject downgrade behavior that opens newer formats for write, hides compatibility failure, or treats read-only export as format support.
Reject any package that uses real user data, real browser profiles, real credentials, or production profile roots before owner-approved policy exists.
Reject sync, credential storage, credential safety, account, collaboration, or cloud behavior claims based only on this schema-package template.
Reject production profile-format, user-data handling readiness, data-loss safety, daily-driver, beta, stable, or release-readiness claims based only on this template.
Reject promotion when storage, privacy-data, product, security, release-operations, quality, and program owner review is absent or stale.
